chapter_context.summary: Eight-year-old Josiah reigns righteously; he repairs the Temple, where Hilkiah finds the Book of the Law; Josiah repents, and the prophetess Huldah foretells judgment but peace for Josiah.

outline.text: Eight-Year-Old Josiah's Righteous Reign in Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Eight-Year-Old Josiah's Righteous Reign in Judah (22:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Josiah Orders the Repair of the Temple
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Josiah Orders the Repair of the Temple (22:3-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Hilkiah Finds the Book of the Law; Read to Josiah; Josiah Rends His Clothes
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hilkiah Finds the Book of the Law; Read to Josiah; Josiah Rends His Clothes (22:8-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Huldah the Prophetess Consulted; Confirms Wrath but Promises Peace to Josiah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Huldah the Prophetess Consulted; Confirms Wrath but Promises Peace to Josiah (22:14-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 22 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
